summary: Provision new Lightsail instances. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — day-2 operations on existing instances land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new Lightsail instance
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Lightsail instance; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Lightsail instance as a complete specification: zone, blueprint, plan size, and network. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: instance_name
params.label: Instance name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like marketing-site. Names the instance in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: availability_zone
params.label: Availability zone
params.help: Pick a zone already in use by the estate's own subnets — Lightsail has no VPC/subnet concept, so the instance simply launches in whichever zone you choose.

params.name: blueprint_id
params.label: Blueprint
params.help: The starting OS image or application stack.

params.name: bundle_id
params.label: Plan size
params.help: The plan size — fixes vCPU, RAM, storage and the monthly data-transfer allowance.

params.name: ip_address_type
params.label: IP address type
params.help: dualstack also assigns an IPv6 address alongside the IPv4 one.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this instance does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this instance.

decisions: A specific Lightsail key pair (its own namespace, separate from EC2 key pairs) is an engineer decision — leave blank for the account default
A custom user-data bootstrap script is an engineer follow-up added after creation
Static IP, disk attachments, and load-balancer attachment are engineer follow-ups after the instance exists
